gaps.description: Organs critical to MSP enforcement—Senate (#220), Nomos (#200), Arbiter (#190)—are repeatedly missing heartbeats and entering DEGRADED state. This instability risks Nomos failing to adjudicate APs, Arbiter failing scope queries, and Senate being unable to amend policy, rendering the governance system nonfunctional.

The MSP mandates that amendments originate from Senate-Archivist writes, Senate-Legislator drafts, and Senate-Rollout executes. The world state shows only a generic Senate organ, no designated Archivist, Legislator, or Rollout personas/organs. Without these, no MSP amendment can occur, leaving the DIO in permissive mode indefinitely.

The Bill of Rights, the constitutional document that the MSP translates into rules, is a scaffold with all articles undefined. Without a human-authored BoR, scope queries by Arbiter have no actual constraints, and any future MSP rules lack a superior legal frame. The system operates without its fundamental identity.

raw_responses:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-BOR-001",
      "title": "Bill of Rights scaffold lacks human-authored content, leaving the DIO without a constitutional perimeter",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights (version 1.0.0-seed) exists only as a scaffold with all six articles marked '[To be defined by human principal]'. The Arbiter (#190) verifies this document by hash at every scope query, but the scaffold contains no substantive constraints, scopes, prohibitions, or identity definitions. As a result, no governance-class event can be evaluated against a meaningful enterprise perimeter, and the Mission Statement Protocol cannot translate human intent into machine-actionable rules because no such intent has been articulated. All APs processed under this BoR are effectively ungoverned, violating the MSP’s stated purpose and the hierarchical compliance architecture.",
      "grounded_in": [
        "BoR Article I — Scope of Enterprise [undefined]",
        "BoR Article II — Prohibited Activities [undefined]",
        "BoR Article III — Core Identity [undefined]",
        "BoR Article IV — Human Authority Reservation [undefined]",
        "BoR Article V — Amendment Protocol [undefined]",
        "BoR Article VI — Interpretation Rules [undefined]",
        "MSP section 'Relationship to the Bill of Rights' — requires a valid BoR for conflict resolution and rule subordination"
      ],
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "high",
      "risk": "Without a complete BoR, the DIO operates in a legal and ethical vacuum. All actions are potentially out-of-scope, unsafe, or unauthorized. Human oversight boundaries are undefined, making the enterprise susceptible to mission drift, regulatory violation, and principal-agent breakdown. The Arbiter's scope gate becomes a meaningless formality, and Nomos’s compliance gates collapse to default-permissive evaluation with no upper bound."
    }
  ]
}

{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "id": "gap-001",
      "gap": "The Bill of Rights (Article I – Scope of Enterprise) is entirely undefined. Without a declared enterprise scope, memory/context formation cannot be aligned to any strategic boundary. All incoming context is treated as potentially relevant, causing information overload, distraction from core mission, and uncontrolled memory growth. Recent context includes routine backup results (e.g., NAS mount failures) and git sync notices that, absent scope, consume strategic attention and storage without justification.",
      "grounded_in": "BoR Article I — Scope of Enterprise [undefined]",
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 5,
      "impact": 5,
      "priority": 25
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-002",
      "gap": "The Dream Cycle health is ‘never_run’ and scheduled execution is ‘disabled’. Memory consolidation (Phase 2) has not executed, nor is it governed by any strategic objective. The MSP has zero rules regarding memory lifecycle, consolidation frequency, or quality thresholds. This gap allows memory to fragment, duplicates to accumulate, and strategic knowledge to degrade, directly undermining the ability to form a coherent operational picture aligned with any future strategic mandate.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP Rules section [empty]; BoR Article II — Prohibited Activities [undefined — no prohibition on neglecting memory maintenance]",
      "criticality": 4,
      "urgency": 5,
      "impact": 4,
      "priority": 20
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-003",
      "gap": "The MSP lacks any genome references or organ-specific constraints. Consequently, memory formation rules are uniform across all organs, and there is no strategic differentiation of what each organ must remember. The recent memory shows over 50 ‘llm-ops’ entries but only a handful for coretex, graphheight, etc. With no organ-level memory charter, critical architectural identity memories risk being diluted by high-volume operational noise.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP — Genome References [none listed]; BoR Article III — Core Identity [undefined]",
      "criticality": 4,
      "urgency": 4,
      "impact": 4,
      "priority": 16
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-004",
      "gap": "Memory embedding coverage is 72% (69 with, 27 without). Without a BoR or MSP objective defining minimum retrieval quality or memory integrity standards, there is no strategic pressure to close this gap. 28% of stored memories are effectively unsearchable, weakening the strategic value of the memory base and making it impossible to guarantee that mission-critical knowledge is retrievable under time pressure.",
      "grounded_in": "BoR Article VI — Interpretation Rules [undefined]; MSP Purpose — ‘machine-actionable set of objectives’ lacking measurable quality objectives",
      "criticality": 3,
      "urgency": 4,
      "impact": 3,
      "priority": 12
    },
    {
      "id": "gap-005",
      "gap": "Human Authority Reservation (BoR Article IV) is undefined. This gap means there is no strategic guidance on which memory/context decisions require human confirmation, and which can be automated. The current Radiant slice shows context blocks persisting for many days (e.g., backup failures from 2026-05-03 still live) without any human-in-the-loop prioritization or pruning rule. This creates a risk that the system will autonomously discard strategically vital memories while retaining low-value operational noise, absent authority boundaries.",
      "grounded_in": "BoR Article IV — Human Authority Reservation [undefined]",
      "criticality": 4,
      "urgency": 3,
      "impact": 4,
      "priority": 12
    }
  ]
}

{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "id": "GAP-001",
      "title": "MSP Rules Section Empty — Default-Permissive Operational Mode",
      "description": "The MSP currently contains no rules (seed version). This means the entire DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation, with no formal SLAs, runtime targets, cadence baselines, or operational constraints. All action proposals are authorized without operational compliance checks beyond the Arbiter scope gate. The repeated spine transitions (DEGRADED ↔ ALIVE) are ungoverned; no rule defines acceptable behaviour for missed pongs, recovery windows, or organ availability.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP § Rules (currently \"none\")",
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 5,
      "impact": 5,
      "priority": 125,
      "notes": "This is the foundational operational gap. Until the Senate publishes substantive rules, the system cannot enforce any operational discipline."
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-002",
      "title": "Bill of Rights Scaffold — No Scope or Prohibited Activities Defined",
      "description": "The BoR remains a scaffold with all articles marked \"To be defined\". Without an enterprise scope (Article I) and prohibited activities (Article II), the Arbiter scope gate (#190) is effectively non-functional. Even though the Arbiter verifies the BoR at every scope query, there is no content to enforce. During periods of spine instability (mass DEGRADED events), no constitutional boundaries restrict what automated organs may do.",
      "grounded_in": "BoR Articles I & II (currently undefined)",
      "criticality": 5,
      "urgency": 5,
      "impact": 5,
      "priority": 125,
      "notes": "Constitutional vacuum; all organs operate without human-defined limits, which is especially dangerous when the spine shows rapid state flapping."
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-003",
      "title": "Undefined Spine Health-Check Cadence and Pong Timeout",
      "description": "The spine-state slice shows a repeating pattern every ~30 seconds: all 25+ organs simultaneously transition from ALIVE to DEGRADED (missed_pong), then recover ~2–3 seconds later (pong_recovered). This indicates a heartbeat mechanism without explicitly codified cadence, timeout thresholds, or dampening rules. In the absence of an MSP rule specifying acceptable pong latency, maximum consecutive missed pongs before alarm, or required recovery time, there is no objective measure of health. The observed jitter may mask genuine partial failures.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP § Rules (expected to contain Spine health-check parameters, currently empty)",
      "criticality": 4,
      "urgency": 4,
      "impact": 3,
      "priority": 48,
      "notes": "Without baseline parameters, the DEGRADED state is temporarily ignored. Frequent flaps could hide cascading issues or cause upstream consumers to react unpredictably."
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-004",
      "title": "No Organ Availability SLA or Degradation Tolerance Defined",
      "description": "The spine transitions show all organs entering DEGRADED nearly simultaneously, and recovering together. There is no MSP rule defining a maximum DEGRADED duration, maximum permitted DEGRADED frequency per window, or minimum required ALIVE percentage. Consequently, there is no operational target for organ availability. If a genuine outage persisted, the system would not have an SLA to measure against, and automated responses (e.g., rollback, alerting) would have no trigger thresholds.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP § Rules (availability targets, expected but absent)",
      "criticality": 4,
      "urgency": 3,
      "impact": 4,
      "priority": 48,
      "notes": "The recovery is currently fast, but without an SLA, prolonged degradation may go unnoticed or unactionable."
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-005",
      "title": "Genome References Absent — No Organ-Specific Operating Rules",
      "description": "The MSP Genome References section is empty. Genome documents are intended to provide organ-specific operational rules and risk-tier-specific constraints. Without them, the MSP cannot differentiate between organs. For instance, Spine's own health-check organ (or any other) has no tail-specific throttling, retry, or circuit-breaking rules. This gap amplifies the lack of rules in the MSP proper.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP § Genome References (currently \"none\")",
      "criticality": 3,
      "urgency": 3,
      "impact": 3,
      "priority": 27,
      "notes": "While less urgent than the missing MSP rules, this gap prevents fine-grained operational control that could mitigate the observed spine flapping behavior."
    }
  ]
}

```json
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-001",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights scaffold is entirely undefined – no Articles exist for scope of enterprise, core identity, human authority reservation, or prohibited activities. As a result, the DIO lacks its constitutional boundary and cannot evaluate whether any action respects the human principal's relational authority. The MSP is strictly subordinate to the BoR, but with a null BoR the entire normative chain is void.",
      "msp_section_reference": "MSP – Relationship to the Bill of Rights (void-on-its-face clause for BoR conflicts)",
      "bor_article_reference": "BoR Articles I–VI (all empty)",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "extreme",
      "priority_score": 10
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-002",
      "description": "The MSP contains no rules governing person-attention obligations – no commitments to response latency, availability window, or notification escalation for the human principal when a BoR conflict PEM is raised. The amendment protocol assumes a human will respond to HOM mailbox notifications, but no MSP rule enforces or monitors that attention loop, leaving the human principal’s relational coverage ungoverned.",
      "msp_section_reference": "MSP – Amendment Protocol (human approval step)",
      "bor_article_reference": "BoR Article IV (Human Authority Reservation – undefined)",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority_score": 8
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-003",
      "description": "The Minder sector (the peer registry, observation engine, and person-domain logic) is acknowledged in observations as not instantiated in the official architecture. While some Minder data exists, there is no Genome reference or MSP governance rule that activates, verifies, or feeds Minder outputs into Nomos compliance gates. Person-attention coverage is thus an ungoverned sidecar, not an integral part of the DIO’s operational constitution.",
      "msp_section_reference": "MSP – Genome References (organ‑specific rules absent for Minder)",
      "bor_article_reference": "BoR Article III (Core Identity – could embed person‑sector identity, but undefined)",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority_score": 7
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-004",
      "description": "The HIPPOCAMPUS recent-conversations array is empty, meaning the system has no short‑term episodic memory of recent interactions with the human principal. While not mandated by the MSP, this absence degrades relational attention by removing the context that allows the system to recall recent topics, deferred decisions, or evolving preferences, undermining the responsiveness expected in a human‑in‑the‑loop governance model.",
      "msp_section_reference": "MSP – implicit in maintaining effective amendment response (human principal mailbox)",
      "bor_article_reference": "BoR Article V (Amendment Protocol – nil definition of interaction continuity)",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "medium",
      "priority_score": 4
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-005",
      "description": "The MSP does not define how the human principal’s observed preferences (e.g., ‘ask before infrastructure changes’, ‘testing after every change’) translate from Minder observations into constitutional constraints within the BoR or MSP. Without a formal path for person‑attention data to seed constitutional rules, the system cannot evolve its relational commitments in a governable way.",
      "msp_section_reference": "MSP – Purpose (translates BoR into actionable objectives) – no feed‑forward from Minder observations",
      "bor_article_reference": "BoR Article I (Scope of Enterprise – undefined, cannot incorporate person‑derived constraints)",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "low",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority_score": 5
    }
  ]
}
```
